75233.body:

‚ÄúConsumption is the sole end and purpose of all production.‚ÄĚ
_‚ÄĒ __Adam Smith__ _



----------View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/b6a856a4-7311-4f70-8a0a-038ea903e108/image.png?t=1782769950)
Caption: 

# The new currency of want

The [Galaxy M9 SUV](https://www.edmunds.com/car-news/2026-geely-galaxy-m9-hybrid-chinese-suv-tested.html) is loaded with features standard in China but unheard of in the US.

The seats are leather, heated, ventilated, massaging, and fully reclinable. With the press of a button, all three rows (except the driver‚Äôs seat) fold flat to create cargo space.

A built-in refrigerator can be set to cold for drinks or to warm for food. Exterior speakers play songs or shout greetings at passersby. The 30-inch dashboard display has 6k resolution and ‚ÄúiPhone-like‚ÄĚ responsiveness.¬†

There‚Äôs a built-in karaoke machine.

It‚Äôs highly capable, too. The plug-in hybrid has a driving range of 808 miles. It accelerates from 0 to 60 in 4.2 seconds ‚ÄĒ faster than a BMW X5. The cabin registers just 32.5 decibels of noise when at idle ‚ÄĒ quieter than a Rolls-Royce Spectre.

The cost of such luxury? $25,000.

A reviewer for Edmunds says the M9 is comparable to what you‚Äôd get for more than twice the price in the US. 

But I think that‚Äôs underselling how left-behind US car buyers are becoming. And overcharged.

The BYD Great Tang SUV, for example, goes 620 miles on a single battery charge. In the US, the¬† longest-ranged car is the Lucid Air Grand Touring, which manages just 512 miles. 

The Lucid Air starts at $138,000. The BYD costs $38,000.

The BYD also responds to voice commands. You can tell it to roll the windows down 25%, for example, or order a coffee at the next Starbucks for you. (And then to drive you there, of course.)

In a competitor's EV, you can even tell the car to turn itself into a bedroom (pictured above).¬†

‚ÄúTransform your XPENG model's cabin into a comfortable, spacious sleeping area, allowing you to take a break and re-energize on long journeys,‚ÄĚ their website [says](https://www.xpeng.com/).

XPENG models start at $17,000.

XPENG‚Äôs come with an option to put a drone on the roof. Tell the car to launch and watch the drone‚Äôs video feed on your display ‚ÄĒ to s

75232.subject: From Waltons To Rockefellers, These Are America‚Äôs Richest Families
75232.body:
Forbes P.M.

–ź–≤—ā–ĺ—Ä: Forbes


–ź–≤—ā–ĺ—Ä: Forbes

    
    
    –ü—Ä–ĺ—á–ł—ā–į–Ļ—ā–Ķ —Ü—é —Ā—ā–į—ā—ā—é –Ĺ–į LinkedIn, —Č–ĺ–Ī –Ņ—Ä–ł—Ē–ī–Ĺ–į—ā–ł—Ā—Ź –ī–ĺ –ĺ–Ī–≥–ĺ–≤–ĺ—Ä–Ķ–Ĺ–Ĺ—Ź

https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/from-waltons-rockefellers-americas-richest-families-forbes-magazine-8e0lc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3B17zPbgpSQJmiPb6AGUtr7w%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=0yxsdoP2yEosk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mqzq2cg0~hy-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mqzq2cg0-hy
  
Good evening, As America‚Äôs 250th anniversary approaches, Forbes is celebrating the entrepreneurs behind some of the most successful businesses‚ÄĒand the largest family fortunes‚ÄĒin the country. That includes everyone from the Hughes family (estimated fortune: $14.7 billion) behind Public Storage, who are now in their third generation, to the oldest clan on the list, the Du Ponts ($22 billion), who founded what became the chemical giant bearing their name in 1802, and are now in their ninth generation. The combined net worth of this year‚Äôs group is $1.9 trillion, nearly $600 billion more than the total two years ago. Here‚Äôs who made the 2026 list. Thanks for reading! Kris Kornbluth Assistant Editor, Platforms & Curation More Recommended Reads Getty Images Ellison‚Äôs Net Worth Plunges $100 Billion‚ÄĒFalling Below Zuckerberg As World‚Äôs Seventh Richest The switch-up follows another rise in Meta‚Äôs stock and a nearly monthlong downturn in Oracle shares. Associated Press A Testy Musk Attacks Claims DOGE‚Äôs Cuts Killed Children. But Studies Paint Grim Picture. Refugees International cited USAID‚Äôs closure as the ‚Äúmost prominent manifestation‚ÄĚ of a downturn in international humanitarian aid.

75231.subject: Webinar: Your Retirement Account Can Do More Than You Think
75231.body:
A step-by-step guide to investing with your IRA or 401(k) funds

IRA Webinar (https://dxszHp04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DR+113/dxszHp04/VXcc4D8BT2yxV-JGFD5hWMbkVhPn1w5QVCGlN70SksF3lcq-W69sMD-6lZ3lDW6SM-rF75wZ9MW5cgx7K20S9CkW4VfbjN6zrNwWN8jbgFNThqYLW1M3nZG82MvfjVft5C31M1dJBV21FSw29rGt_W6lSmNM5ry38nW1DWN4W4WWyWJW5WBSMJ2Rsls_W5RwfkD8LLbwvW5VHdj_5xLjsvN71cBfLYd71LW7B9MNC1Mqrk4W4ScfMH7DSHpXW7WVnvt6MtT4qV7hs4b1jtXJhW2fh4fq1K3XFbW2LBKtg8w0wmqW4z9GX63BkFcCdRkBjR04 )

Hi Oleg,

One of the most common questions I hear from investors:

‚ÄúCan I use my retirement savings to invest in pre-IPO opportunities?‚ÄĚ

The answer is ‚ÄúYes,‚ÄĚ and I‚Äôm hosting a webinar next week to walk you through how it works.

Join me on Thursday, July 2, at 2:00 PM ET / 11:00 AM PT for a step-by-step guide on investing with a self-directed IRA. We‚Äôll cover:

- Pre-IPO in 2026: Why 86% of wealth managers are increasing their private allocation.¬Ļ
- The 50/30/20 Portfolio: Why BlackRock‚Äôs Larry Fink is advocating for a new standard.¬≤
- Potential Tax Advantages: How gains can be tax-deferred or potentially tax-free.¬≥
- Setting Up Your Account: From rolling over funds to browsing opportunities.

More than 800 investors have opened StartEngine IRA accounts, putting over $17 million to work in pre-IPO opportunities for companies like Anthropic, OpenAI, and Stripe.‚Āī

Whether you‚Äôre ready to join them or just exploring your options, this session will give you a clear roadmap for putting your retirement dollars to work.

Register Here
(https://dxszHp04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DR+113/dxszHp04/VXcc4D8BT2yxV-JGFD5hWMbkVhPn1w5QVCGlN70SksF3lcq-W69sMD-6lZ3lDW6SM-rF75wZ9MW5cgx7K20S9CkW4VfbjN6zrNwWN8jbgFNThqYLW1M3nZG82MvfjVft5C31M1dJBV21FSw29rGt_W6lSmNM5ry38nW1DWN4W4WWyWJW5WBSMJ2Rsls_W5RwfkD8LLbwvW5VHdj_5xLjsvN71cBfLYd71LW7B9MNC1Mqrk4W4ScfMH7DSHpXW7WVnvt6MtT4qV7hs4b1jtXJhW2fh4fq1K3XFbW2LBKtg8w0wmqW4z9GX63BkFcCdRkBjR04 )

Equity Trust Company is the custodian of StartEngine IRAs. StartEngine Crowdfunding, Inc. covers IRA account fees if you invest $20,000 or more per year; otherwise, you are responsible for all applicable fees annually. See IRA Fees (https://dxszHp04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DR+113/dxszHp04/VXcc4D8BT2yxV-JGFD5hWMbkVhPn1w5QVCGlN70Skv63lcq-W95jsWP6lZ3pRVj-xz76m7cgwVDltvM1M7435W6lvZQX7wP08kW7QYyfb3v114HW3Zpd8S1QqknTW7Bmw_Y6M0BFqW880Bn18kt971W9g-tDD1x72ZCW81N7074_LQX0W8DQWkV2jB4zYV4JYWK1Br0h2W2kRmYj6lWMwkW8qsbFJ3_KMmQW7-JXN16bLZ_tW5JqFcX6L5hvfW95xdrY24R3gCW73wJb05HXG51W7YZV103LGhRmW6CClgn5WbwCbW3_0L3V8pYBFHW1Yxssz4gg-LrW3SHCWh41SrBHW92zvjk5Vsld3W5FVfG74P942ZW3gsfqK5sdqYtW8S8BsW6sKL0CW8CNjM728pJw5W3lV5qx9k6GqJW1gpl4j6zJRJnW3FZMcg2r353Zf72mw4n04 ) and footnote 1 for more details.

Best,

James Munro

Senior Private Equity Associate

StartEngine

75230.subject: ūüĒ∑ The Daily: CZ can't make sense of Saylor's STRC
75230.body:
Strategy pauses bitcoin buys, establishes $1B digital credit repurchase program as USD reserve tops $2.5B | India's USDT premium tops 8.5% as crypto remittance crackdown squeezes stablecoin supply | BlackRock's Aladdin platform adds deeper support for Ethena's stablecoin products | Ethereum zkRollup project Loopring sunsets DEX, citing lack of meaningful adoption |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Jason Shubnell

Presented by

BitGo ( https://www.bitgo.com/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Happy Monday! *Bitcoin* is hovering around $60,000 as analysts warn ( https://www.theblock.co/post/406516/bitcoin-clings-to-key-support-level-as-weekly-us-spot-etf-outflows-hit-1-8b-and-fed-rate-hike-bets-mount-analysts?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) that heavy ETF outflows, rising demand for downside protection, and renewed *Fed* -hike bets are keeping pressure on crypto markets.

In today's newsletter, *Binance* founder *Changpeng "CZ" Zhao* says its *MiCA* application was "fully compliant," *Strategy* pauses its *bitcoin* buys and establishes a $1 billion digital credit repurchase program, *India's USDT* premium tops 8.5% amid a crypto remittance crackdown, and more.

Meanwhile, *Galaxy Research* cut ( https://www.theblock.co/post/406497/galaxy-cuts-clarity-act-passage-odds-50?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) its odds for *Clarity Act* passage in 2026 to 50%, citing *Senate* calendar concerns.

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read ‚ÄĒ and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*ūüá™ūüáļ Binance founder CZ says MiCA application was 'fully compliant' and near approval before political forces intervened*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/406554/binance-founder-cz-mica-application-fully-compliant-near-approval-political-forces-intervened?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Speaking on the inaugural episode of *The Block's* new daily show, The Starting Block , *Binance* founder *Changpeng "CZ" Zhao* said his understanding is that the crypto exchange's *MiCA* application was fully compliant and near approval from at least one EU regulator before political forces intervened, prompting last week's withdrawal.

* CZ said two EU countries wanted Binance in a "bidding war" over the license, but there were "other forces ... politics, et cetera" that opposed it, declining to

75227.subject:
Data & Insights: BTC ETF Historic Redemptions; Strategy's Stretching
 Treasury
75227.body:
The Data and Insights newsletter brings the highlights of the week from The Block‚Äôs Data Dashboard

( https://www.theblock.co/data/crypto-markets/spot )

Presented by:

BitGo ( https://www.bitgo.com/ )

Gm data enthusiasts! This week, we‚Äôre watching the World Cup‚Äôs round of 16 and how it‚Äôs continued to drive volume across prediction markets. Our team recently published a new report titled " Gate CrossEx: An Exchange-Native Answer to the Institutional Capital Fragmentation Problem ( https://www.theblock.co/post/404882/gate-crossex-an-exchange-native-answer-to-the-institutional-capital-fragmentation-problem )." We recommend giving it a check.

This week, we‚Äôre taking a look at what‚Äôs going on with the ETF flows and Strategy‚Äôs STRC. We‚Äôll also look at ETH volumes, RWAs, and Bitcoin price. So grab a drink, take a seat, and let's dive in.

1: Can't Stop Flowing

( https://www.theblock.co/data/etfs/bitcoin-etf/spot-bitcoin-etf-flows )

Chart via The Block's Data Dashboard

*Key Insights* provided by The Block Research

* This is a section from The Block's The Funding newsletter ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters ) , where also cover venture fundings developments and topics across the broader institutional crypto space ‚ÄĒ including Wall Street, public companies, institutional products and corporate adoption.
* Spot bitcoin ETFs notched a seventh straight week of net outflows, extending the longest weekly redemption stretch since the products launched in January 2024.

* BTC traded as low as $58,189 intraday this week, its weakest print since September 2024, as a $692 million single-day outflow on June 25 marked the largest daily redemption since May and added pressure to a thin liquidity window.

* IBIT has continued to account for the bulk of both sides of the flow data, underscoring how concentrated the ETF complex has become. Smaller issuers like FBTC and GBTC have bled more consistently on a percentage basis, even as BlackRock's fund occasionally posts standalone green days.
* Despite the redemption pressure, cumulative trading volume across spot bitcoin ETFs has crossed $2 trillion since launch, a reminder that two-way trading activity and net asset retention are distinct signals. The wrapper remains liquid and actively used even as net demand turns negative.

See the Interactive Chart ( https://www.theblock.co/data/etfs/bitcoin-etf/spot-bitcoin-etf-flows )

2: Stretched Thin

( https://www.theblock.co/data/crypto-markets/public-companies/strategys-preferred-stocks-volumes )

Chart via The Block's Data Dashboard

*Key Insights* provided by The Block Research

* Strategy's STRC preferred stock, nicknamed "Stretch" for its design to hold near its $100 par value, has drifted into the mid-$70s, exposing strain in the capital structure underpinning Michael Saylor's bitcoin accumulation model.

* STRC's dividend cash coverage has compressed sharply, from a previously cited multi-year runway to under a year, as an

75226.subject: Too much sleep might be as bad as too little
75226.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MjJ9AAcAAAAAA1jgGjDO9M953LJt01EWIc6UOBBwJZ4dmbh3zDzuhtrR0UM4ZtomhOIGcRJEwfnis8CVOkS0xfWIMp2S-GPv4fx6AAJQiuUD-YNv6S7vjps 

June 29th 2026

 

 
 
 
The Economist Today

 Our best journalism, handpicked each day


 
 
 

 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MjJ9AAcAAAAAA1jgGjDPiDA7tn1dAFBdk44bNOr0xPgDX_Jx3xuQQwIPpnNMeifiojd_w1h7sH3moYf7dkicMm2e6uq6jxsZwwiGBf8tzGXxTQ75ig70ur4 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MjJ9AAcAAAAAA1jgGjDQlpNjHAQ_5dEkKi3rV0Qf59EgHvH1rtCM-4nb22fI5mLs84FfmPz-BllPd6mN7pikB7xp9-7SALdgwfgtnItWZ9GHkm-Ydcse6Mc 


 


 
 

Stephanie Studer

Senior digital editor



 If you luxuriate in long stretches of sleep, here’s some science that might keep you up at night: too many hours may be as bad for you as too few. The evidence suggests a U-shaped relationship between health and sleep duration, with an optimal middle range. Reassuringly, there is 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MjJ9AAcAAAAAA1jgGjDRx5SZ-A27v-wJmZNeBTE6nNLwxxSbznzEbt_gzCOJ8hPtOhn1zf0ChocACfoJ5HeVv-IQGWe2ZvLrYVR2ORp22OGIi-eJVg 
more to sleep  
 than simple duration.


 Andy Burnham today set out for the first time what he wants to do if—or rather, when—he becomes prime minister. There were hints of radicalism. But so far his plans look a lot like those 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB
